```
import tempfile
import mlflow


def main() -> None:
    old_tracking_uri = mlflow.get_tracking_uri()
    old_registry_uri = mlflow.get_registry_uri()

    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="mlflow-prompt-cache-repro-") as tmpdir:
        uri = f"file://{tmpdir}"
        prompt_name = "my_prompt"

        mlflow.set_tracking_uri(uri)
        mlflow.set_registry_uri(uri)

        try:
            mlflow.genai.register_prompt(
                name=prompt_name,
                template="Answer: {{question}}",

            print("after update, latest.model_config =", stale_latest.model_config)
            print("after update, version=1.model_config =", fresh_version.model_config)
        finally:
            mlflow.set_tracking_uri(old_tracking_uri)
            mlflow.set_registry_uri(old_registry_uri)


if __name__ == "__main__":
```

mlflow/mlflow#21521.body:
<!-- issue-warning -->
> [!WARNING]
> Before submitting a PR, please make sure that:
> - A maintainer has triaged this issue and applied the `ready` label
> - This issue has no assignee
> - No duplicate PR exists
>
> PRs not meeting these requirements may be automatically closed.

## Description

The `-full` Docker image (`ghcr.io/mlflow/mlflow:VERSION-full`) documents support for "Cloud storage integrations (Azure Blob)" and includes an Azure Blob Storage example in `docker/README.md`, but the image does not actually install `azure-storage-blob`, `azure-identity`, or `adlfs`.

## Reproduction

```bash
docker run --rm --entrypoint bash ghcr.io/mlflow/mlflow:v3.10.1-full -c "pip show azure-storage-blob"
# WARNING: Package(s) not found: azure-storage-blob

docker run --rm --entrypoint bash ghcr.io/mlflow/mlflow:v3.10.1-full -c   "python -c \"from mlflow.store.artifact.azure_blob_artifact_repo import AzureBlobArtifactRepository; AzureBlobArtifactRepository('wasbs://cont@acct.blob.core.windows.net/path')\""
# ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'azure.storage'
```

## Root cause

`docker/Dockerfile.full` installs `mlflow[extras,db,gateway,genai]`, but none of these extras include `azure-storage-blob`, `azure-identity`, or `adlfs`. The import in `AzureBlobArtifactRepository.__init__()` is lazy, so `mlflow server` starts successfully — but any attempt to store or retrieve artifacts via a `wasbs://` URI fails at runtime.

The image does include `azure-core` and `azure-mgmt-storage` (via `azureml-core`), but these are management-plane packages, not the data-plane packages needed for blob I/O.

## Expected behavior

The `-full` image should work out of the box with Azure Blob Storage artifact URIs, as documented in `docker/README.md`.

## Proposed fix

Add `azure-storage-blob`, `azure-identity`, and `adlfs` to the pip install in `docker/Dockerfile.full`. See PR #21520.

## Environment

- MLflow version: 3.10.1
- Docker image: `ghcr.io/mlflow/mlflow:v3.10.1-full`

### Summary

Langchain's audio content is not displayed correctly on the trace UI. This is because Langchain's audio content is not normalized to AudioContentPart when logging span inputs/outputs. The content is passed through as-is in langchain/utils/chat.py. When content is a list of dicts (multimodal), LangChain's standardized "type": "audio" blocks don't match MLflow's expected "type": "input_audio" format.  Refer to how ImageType is normalized in anthropic/chat.py

### Issues Policy acknowledgement

- [x] I have read and agree to submit bug reports in accordance with the [issues policy](https://www.github.com/mlflow/mlflow/blob/master/ISSUE_POLICY.md)

### Where did you encounter this bug?

Local machine

### MLflow version

- Client: master (`d3777`)


### System information

- **OS Platform and Distribution (e.g., Linux Ubuntu 16.04)**: WSL 2 Ubuntu 22.04
- **Python version**: 3.10.12


### Describe the problem

MLflow MCP currently generates incorrect tool schemas for CLI arguments that accept multiple values. For example, `mlflow runs link-traces` accepts repeated `--trace-id` flags and `mlflow models update-pip-requirements` accepts one or more positional requirement strings, but the published MCP schemas expose both `trace_ids` and `requirement_strings` as  string instead of arrays. This makes the MCP contract inconsistent with the underlying CLI behavior and can cause MCP clients to construct invalid tool calls.

### Additional affected cases

This does not appear to be limited to the two examples above. The same schema issue also affects other MCP-exposed commands with repeatable or variadic params, including:

  - `mlflow runs create --tags`
  - `mlflow models predict --env`
  - deployment commands with repeatable `--config`, such as `create`, `update`, `delete`, `run-local`, `create-endpoint`, and `update-endpoint`

### Potential fix

  A minimal fix would be to update `mlflow/mcp/server.py:get_input_schema()` so that Click params with `multiple=True` or `nargs = -1` are emitted as array schemas rather than scalar strings, while preserving the underlying item type.

### Tracking information

<!-- PLEASE KEEP BACKTICKS AND CHECK PREVIEW -->
```shell
REPLACE_ME
```


### Code to reproduce issue

<!-- PLEASE KEEP BACKTICKS AND CHECK PREVIEW -->
```
import json

from mlflow.mcp.server import get_input_schema
from mlflow.models.cli import commands as model_commands
from mlflow.runs import commands as run_commands


def _print_schema(title: str, user_input_shape: str, actual_schema: dict) -> None:
    print(f"\n[{title}]")
    print(f"expected MCP schema ({user_input_shape}):")
    print(json.dumps({"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}}, indent=2, sort_keys=True))
    print("actual MCP schema:")
    print(json.dumps(actual_schema, indent=2, sort_keys=True))


def main() -> int:
    link_traces_cmd = run_commands.commands["link-traces"]
    link_traces_schema = get_input_schema(link_traces_cmd.params)["properties"]["trace_ids"]
    _print_schema(
        "runs link-traces :: trace_ids",
        "repeatable `--trace-id` input",
        link_traces_schema,
    )

    update_reqs_cmd = model_commands.commands["update-pip-requirements"]
    update_reqs_schema = get_input_schema(update_reqs_cmd.params)["properties"]["requirement_strings"]
    _print_schema(
        "models update-pip-requirements :: requirement_strings",
        "one or more positional requirement strings",
        update_reqs_schema,
    )
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

<!-- issue-warning -->

### Issues Policy acknowledgement

- [x] I have read and agree to submit bug reports in accordance with the [issues policy](https://www.github.com/mlflow/mlflow/blob/master/ISSUE_POLICY.md)

### Where did you encounter this bug?

Local machine

### MLflow version

Client: 3.10.1 
Tracking server: 3.10.1



### System information

OS Platform and Distribution: Linux 5.10.0-38-cloud-amd64 (Debian) 
Python version: 3.11.14



### Describe the problem

When re-registering a scorer (calling .register() on a judge with a name that is already registered for the same experiment), the scorer version is successfully created and committed (version number increments, instructions are updated in the UI), but the call raises a RestException with RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS due to a duplicate permission insert.

**Expected behavior:** Re-registering a scorer with the same name should either succeed silently (the permission already exists) or return the new version without error.

**Actual behavior:** The scorer version is created and committed to the database, but the after_request auth hook attempts to INSERT a new permission record that already exists, hits a UniqueViolation, and raises an MlflowException that overwrites the successful response. The end result is a successful database write paired with a client-side error, which is confusing and inconsistent.

## Root cause

The registration flow is non-atomic across two separate systems.
First, in `mlflow/store/tracking/sqlalchemy_store.py`, `register_scorer()` runs and commits successfully. It handles re-registration correctly by checking if the scorer exists and only creating a new version.
Second, in Flask's after_request hook (`mlflow/server/auth/__init__.py`), the handler `set_can_manage_scorer_permission` unconditionally calls `create_scorer_permission`, which does a raw INSERT. On re-registration, the permission already exists from the first registration, so it violates the `unique_scorer_user` constraint on the `scorer_permissions` table.
The core issue is that Step 1 commits independently before Step 2 runs, so the scorer update persists even though the overall request returns an error.

## Suggested fix

The `set_can_manage_scorer_permission` handler should handle the case where the permission already exists. For example:
```
# In mlflow/server/auth/__init__.py
def set_can_manage_scorer_permission(resp: Response):
    response_message = RegisterScorer.Response()
    parse_dict(resp.json, response_message)
    experiment_id = response_message.experiment_id
    name = response_message.name
    username = authenticate_request().username
    try:
        store.create_scorer_permission(experiment_id, name, username, MANAGE.name)
    except MlflowException:
        pass  # Permission already exists, safe to ignore on re-registration
```
Alternatively, `create_scorer_permission` in `mlflow/server/auth/sqlalchemy_store.py` could use upsert or get-or-create semantics instead of a raw INSERT.


### Tracking information

<!-- PLEASE KEEP BACKTICKS AND CHECK PREVIEW -->
```shell
```


### Code to reproduce issue

<!-- PLEASE KEEP BACKTICKS AND CHECK PREVIEW -->
```
from typing import Literal
import mlflow
from mlflow.genai.judges import make_judge

mlflow.set_experiment("my-experiment")
experiment = mlflow.get_experiment_by_name("my-experiment")
experiment_id = experiment.experiment_id

judge = make_judge(
    name="test_judge",
    instructions="Evaluate {{ outputs }} given {{ inputs }}.",
    feedback_value_type=Literal["supported", "not_supported"],
    model="openai:/gpt-4o-mini",
)

# First registration - succeeds
judge.register(experiment_id=experiment_id)

# Second registration (e.g. with updated instructions) - raises error
# despite successfully creating a new version
updated_judge = make_judge(
    name="test_judge",
    instructions="Updated instructions. Evaluate {{ outputs }} given {{ inputs }}.",
    feedback_value_type=Literal["supported", "not_supported"],
    model="openai:/gpt-4o-mini",
)
updated_judge.register(experiment_id=experiment_id)  # Raises RestException



```
**Note:** This requires MLflow auth to be enabled on the tracking server. The bug is in the auth layer's after_request hook, so it only manifests when authentication is active.

### Stack trace

<!-- PLEASE KEEP BACKTICKS AND CHECK PREVIEW -->
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<cell>", line 2, in <module>
    updated_judge.register(experiment_id=experiment_id)
  ...
  File "/site-packages/mlflow/server/auth/sqlalchemy_store.py", line 435, in create_scorer_permission
    raise MlflowException(
mlflow.exceptions.MlflowException: RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS: Scorer permission
(experiment_id=60, scorer_name=test_judge, username=DistilrMLBatch) already exists.
Error: (psycopg2.errors.UniqueViolation) duplicate key value violates unique constraint "unique_scorer_user"
DETAIL: Key (experiment_id, scorer_name, user_id)=(60, test_judge, 9) already exists.
```


### Other info / logs

The scorer registration succeeds in `mlflow/store/tracking/sqlalchemy_store.py` at `register_scorer()`, which upserts the scorer and creates a new version.
The permission failure occurs in `mlflow/server/auth/__init__.py` at `set_can_manage_scorer_permission`, which is registered as a Flask `after_request` handler. It calls `create_scorer_permission` in `mlflow/server/auth/sqlalchemy_store.py`, which does a blind INSERT without checking for existing permissions.
The unique constraint is defined in `mlflow/server/auth/db/models.py` on the `SqlScorerPermission` model with constraint name `unique_scorer_user`.

Local machine

### System information

- **Python version**: 3.10.18


### Describe the problem

In the OSS prompt registry path, `MlflowClient.register_prompt(tags=...)` documents tags as prompt-version tags, but those tags also get written onto prompt-level tags. As a result, `get_prompt(...).tags` reflects the newest version tags instead of only prompt-level tags.

### Possible Fix
A possible fix would be to keep `register_prompt(tags=...)` version-scoped in the OSS path by storing the provided tags only on the created prompt version, while leaving prompt-level tags to `set_prompt_tag()`. That would stop future leakage without requiring a migration of already-persisted prompt-level tags.

### Tracking information

```shell
REPLACE_ME
```


### Code to reproduce issue

<!-- PLEASE KEEP BACKTICKS AND CHECK PREVIEW -->
```
import tempfile

import mlflow
from mlflow import MlflowClient


def main() -> None:
    tracking_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="mlflow_prompt_repro_")
    uri = f"sqlite:///{tracking_dir}/mlruns.db"
    original_tracking_uri = mlflow.get_tracking_uri()
    original_registry_uri = mlflow.get_registry_uri()

    try:
        mlflow.set_tracking_uri(uri)
        mlflow.set_registry_uri(uri)

        client = MlflowClient(tracking_uri=uri, registry_uri=uri)

        client.register_prompt(
            name="prompt_1",
            template="Hi, {{name}}!",
            tags={"author": "Alice"},
        )
        client.set_prompt_tag("prompt_1", "application", "greeting")

        client.register_prompt(
            name="prompt_1",
            template="Hello, {{name}}!",
            tags={"author": "Bob", "release": "v2"},
        )

        print("prompt_v1.tags =", client.load_prompt("prompt_1", version=1).tags)
        print("prompt_v2.tags =", client.load_prompt("prompt_1", version=2).tags)
        print("get_prompt().tags =", client.get_prompt("prompt_1").tags)
    finally:
        mlflow.set_tracking_uri(original_tracking_uri)
        mlflow.set_registry_uri(original_registry_uri)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

> - A maintainer has triaged this issue and applied the `ready` label

### Issues Policy acknowledgement

- [x] I have read and agree to submit bug reports in accordance with the [issues policy](https://www.github.com/mlflow/mlflow/blob/master/ISSUE_POLICY.md)

### Where did you encounter this bug?

Local machine

### MLflow version

- Client: 3.10.2.dev0


### System information

- **OS Platform and Distribution (e.g., Linux Ubuntu 16.04)**: macOS 26.3.1
- **Python version**: 3.10.20


### Describe the problem

When using MLflow autologging with the OpenAI Agents SDK, running an agent with a custom `RunConfig` causes a crash printed to stderr after the trace is recorded. The error is swallowed silently but pollutes output and indicates object corruption:

```bash
Exception ignored in: <object repr() failed>
AttributeError: 'RunConfig' object has no attribute 'max_turns'
```
**Root cause**: `TraceJSONEncoder.default()` calls `dataclasses.asdict()` to serialize dataclass span attributes. `asdict()` internally calls `copy.deepcopy()` on non-dataclass fields. `RunConfig` holds an `AsyncOpenAI` HTTP client which contains asyncio internals that fail to deepcopy, the copy is created via `__new__` but never fully initialized, and its `__del__` method then crashes.

**Expected**: dataclasses with non-copyable fields serialize gracefully (field values are extracted directly, non-serializable ones fall back to string representation).

**Actual**: partially constructed objects are left alive after the failed deepcopy and crash in `__del__`.

### Tracking information

<!-- PLEASE KEEP BACKTICKS AND CHECK PREVIEW -->
```shell
REPLACE_ME
```


### Code to reproduce issue

<!-- PLEASE KEEP BACKTICKS AND CHECK PREVIEW -->
```python
import gc
import json
from dataclasses import dataclass

from openai import AsyncOpenAI

from mlflow.tracing.utils import TraceJSONEncoder


@dataclass
class Config:
    client: AsyncOpenAI


config = Config(client=AsyncOpenAI(api_key="test"))

# TraceJSONEncoder calls asdict(config) -> copy.deepcopy(AsyncOpenAI instance)
# -> AsyncHttpxClientWrapper created via __new__ (empty __dict__)
# -> deepcopy fails copying asyncio transport internals
# -> partially constructed wrapper is released
# -> GC collects it -> AttributeError in __del__
json.dumps(config, cls=TraceJSONEncoder)
gc.collect()  # force collection to make the error visible immediately

# Expected output to stderr:
# Exception ignored in: <function AsyncHttpxClientWrapper.__del__ at ...>
# Traceback (most recent call last):
#   File ".../openai/_base_client.py", in __del__
#     if self.is_closed:
#   File ".../httpx/_client.py", in is_closed
#     return self._state == ClientState.CLOSED
# AttributeError: 'AsyncHttpxClientWrapper' object has no attribute '_state'
```

### Stack trace

<!-- PLEASE KEEP BACKTICKS AND CHECK PREVIEW -->
```bash
Exception ignored in: <function AsyncHttpxClientWrapper.__del__ at 0x106384ae0>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File ".../.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/openai/_base_client.py", line 1430, in __del__
    if self.is_closed:
       ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File ".../.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/httpx/_client.py", line 202, in is_closed
    return self._state == ClientState.CLOSED
           ^^^^^^^^^^^
AttributeError: 'AsyncHttpxClientWrapper' object has no attribute '_state'
```

Stack call (debugger)
```bash
__del__     openai/_base_client.py:1430
default     mlflow/tracing/utils/__init__.py:104
iterencode  json/encoder.py:258
encode      json/encoder.py:200
dumps       json/__init__.py:238
set         mlflow/entities/span.py:760
set_attribute   mlflow/entities/span.py:424
set_attributes  mlflow/entities/span.py:420
start_span  mlflow/tracing/fluent.py:486
__enter__   contextlib.py:137
_patched_agent_run  mlflow/openai/_agent_tracer.py:280
```

### Other info / logs

**Root cause analysis**

`TraceJSONEncoder.default()` calls `asdict(obj)` for `dataclass` instances. Python's `dataclasses.asdict()` internally calls `copy.deepcopy()` on non-dataclass field values ([source](https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/main/Lib/dataclasses.py)):

```python
# cpython/Lib/dataclasses.py
def _asdict_inner(obj, dict_factory):
    if _is_dataclass_instance(obj):
        ...
    else:
        return copy.deepcopy(obj)  # called on non-dataclass fields
```

When the `dataclass` contains an object with asyncio internals (e.g. `RunConfig` from `openai-agents` containing `OpenAIProvider` -> `AsyncOpenAI` -> `AsyncHttpxClientWrapper`), `deepcopy` follows this sequence:

1. Creates `AsyncHttpxClientWrapper.__new__(AsyncHttpxClientWrapper)` -> `__dict__ = {}`
2. Attempts to deepcopy its `__dict__` (which contains an asyncio transport and locks)
3. Fails, the partially constructed wrapper is released
4. GC collects it -> `__del__` accesses `self._state` -> `AttributeError`
5. Confirmed via debugger: at the time `__del__` fires, `self.__dict__ == {}` and `self._state` raises `AttributeError`.

**Relevant `TraceJSONEncoder.default()` code:**

```python
if is_dataclass(obj):
    try:
        return asdict(obj)       # triggers deepcopy on non-dataclass fields
    except TypeError:            # too narrow: deepcopy can also raise RuntimeError
        pass
```

### What component(s) does this bug affect?

- [ ] `area/tracking`: Tracking Service, tracking client APIs, autologging
- [ ] `area/model-registry`: Model Registry service, APIs, and the fluent client calls for Model Registry
- [ ] `area/scoring`: MLflow model serving, deployment tools, Spark UDFs
- [ ] `area/evaluation`: MLflow model evaluation features, evaluation metrics, and evaluation workflows
- [ ] `area/prompt`: MLflow prompt engineering features, prompt templates, and prompt management
- [x] `area/tracing`: MLflow Tracing features, tracing APIs, and LLM tracing functionality
- [ ] `area/gateway`: MLflow AI Gateway client APIs, server, and third-party integrations
- [ ] `area/projects`: MLproject format, project running backends
- [ ] `area/uiux`: Front-end, user experience, plotting
- [ ] `area/docs`: MLflow documentation pages

### Describe the problem

MLflow's autologging redirects Python-native warnings for threadsafe operations in `mlflow/utils/autologging_utils/logging_and_warnings.py`. It falls back to the original `warnings.showwarning`.

Unfortunately, the original `warnings.showwarning` is backed up at module-level on **import time**. If the user modifies the showwarning function after importing MLflow, like it is common with tools like `logging.captureWarnings`, MLflow essentially overwrites that customization with `ORIGINAL_SHOWWARNING` instead of using the user-updated `warnings.showwarning`. See example below for illustration.

Is it possible to pull the following line into the class `_WarningsController` to prevent definition at import time? That would give the user a chance to define their own showwarning function.

mlflow/mlflow#21693.body:
<!-- issue-warning -->
> [!WARNING]
> Before submitting a PR, please make sure that:
> - A maintainer has triaged this issue and applied the `ready` label
> - This issue has no assignee
> - No duplicate PR exists
>
> PRs not meeting these requirements may be automatically closed.

### Willingness to contribute

Yes. I can contribute this feature independently.

### Proposal Summary

`to_chat_completions_input` in `mlflow.types.responses` has parameter type `list[dict[str, Any] | Message | OutputItem]`, but should accept `Sequence[dict[str, Any] | Message | OutputItem]`. The function never mutates the input so using `list` is unnecessarily restrictive. The same applies to the `prep_msgs_for_cc_llm` wrapper in `mlflow/pyfunc/model.py`, which passes through to the same function.

### Motivation

> #### What is the use case for this feature?

Passing `ResponsesAgentRequest.input` (typed `list[Message | OutputItem]`) directly to `to_chat_completions_input` without a cast or re-annotation.

> #### Why is this use case valuable to support for MLflow users in general?

Anyone building a `ResponsesAgent` that needs to convert the request input to chat completions format encounters this. `ResponsesAgentRequest.input` is `list[Message | OutputItem]`, but `to_chat_completions_input` demands `list[dict[str, Any] | Message | OutputItem]`. These should be compatible without casts.

> #### Why is this use case valuable to support for your project(s) or organization?

We use strict type checking and want to avoid `# type: ignore` or explicit casts for what should be a straightforward call.

> #### Why is it currently difficult to achieve this use case?

`list` is a mutable generic and therefore invariant in Python's type system: `list[A | B]` is not a subtype of `list[A | B | C]`. The type checker must allow the callee to add an element of type `C`, violating the caller's narrower type constraint. The workaround is a cast or widened annotation at every call site.

### Details

**Reproduction:**

```python
from mlflow.types.responses import ResponsesAgentRequest, to_chat_completions_input

request: ResponsesAgentRequest = ...

# Type error: list[Message | OutputItem] is not assignable to
#             list[dict[str, Any] | Message | OutputItem]
cc_messages = to_chat_completions_input(request.input)
```

**Proposed change:**

```diff
# mlflow/types/responses.py
 def to_chat_completions_input(
-    responses_input: list[dict[str, Any] | Message | OutputItem],
+    responses_input: Sequence[dict[str, Any] | Message | OutputItem],
 ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:

 # mlflow/pyfunc/model.py
 @staticmethod
 def prep_msgs_for_cc_llm(
-    responses_input: list[dict[str, Any] | Message | OutputItem],
+    responses_input: Sequence[dict[str, Any] | Message | OutputItem],
 ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
```

This is non-breaking. `list` is a subtype of `Sequence`, so all existing callers continue to work.

- [x] `domain/genai`: LLMs, Agents, and other GenAI-related use cases

### What area(s) of MLflow is this feature request about?

- [x] `area/scoring`: MLflow model serving, deployment tools, Spark UDFs

## Summary

`mlflow-skinny` does not declare `numpy` as a dependency, but `mlflow.anthropic.autolog()` fails with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'numpy'` when numpy is not installed.

## Reproduction

```bash
pip install mlflow-skinny
python -c "import mlflow.anthropic; mlflow.anthropic.autolog()"
```

## Error

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
  File "mlflow/anthropic/__init__.py", line 3, in <module>
    from mlflow.anthropic.autolog import (
  File "mlflow/anthropic/autolog.py", line 5, in <module>
    from mlflow.anthropic.chat import convert_tool_to_mlflow_chat_tool
  File "mlflow/anthropic/chat.py", line 7, in <module>

  File "mlflow/types/__init__.py", line 9, in <module>
    import mlflow.types.llm  # noqa: F401
  File "mlflow/types/llm.py", line 6, in <module>
    from mlflow.types.schema import AnyType, Array, ColSpec, DataType, Map, Object, Property, Schema
  File "mlflow/types/schema.py", line 14, in <module>
    import numpy as np
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'numpy'
```

## Import chain

```
mlflow.anthropic → mlflow.anthropic.autolog → mlflow.anthropic.chat
  → mlflow.types.chat → mlflow.types → mlflow.types.llm → mlflow.types.schema
  → import numpy as np  ← hard import, not guarded
```

## Expected behavior

`mlflow.anthropic.autolog()` should work with `mlflow-skinny` alone, since it's purely an instrumentation/tracing feature that patches the Anthropic SDK to emit OpenTelemetry traces. The anthropic autolog code itself does not use numpy — the dependency is pulled in transitively through `mlflow.types.schema`.

## Suggested fix

Either:
1. Make the `numpy` import in `mlflow/types/schema.py` lazy/conditional
2. Or add `numpy` as a dependency of `mlflow-skinny`

- mlflow-skinny 3.10.0 and 3.10.1
- Python 3.11.13
- Linux x86_64

<!-- issue-warning -->

## Description

Hey! So I've been using the MLflow Assistant (beta) in the UI and I keep running into this error:

```
Error: Unknown message type: rate_limit_event
```

It happens when the assistant is streaming responses and a `rate_limit_event` message comes through from the CLI. The assistant's message parser doesn't know how to handle it and surfaces it as an error to the user.

## Environment

- **MLflow version**: 3.10.0 (also reproduced on 3.10.1)
- **OS**: Linux

## Root Cause

The issue is in `mlflow/assistant/providers/claude_code.py` in the `_parse_message_to_event()` method. The `match` statement handles `user`, `assistant`, `system`, `error`, `result`, and `stream_event` message types, but the default `case _:` branch treats any unrecognized type as an error:

```python
case _:
    return Event.from_error(f"Unknown message type: {message_type}")
```

The CLI can emit `rate_limit_event` messages (and potentially other new types in the future), and these shouldn't crash the assistant — they should just be silently ignored.

## Relevant Prior Art

Interestingly, the exact same issue was already hit and fixed in `anthropics/claude-agent-sdk-python` (#583, #601, #603). The SDK fixed it in **v0.1.40** by returning `None` for unknown message types, which then gets filtered out upstream. The MLflow parser even references the SDK parser in a comment (line 471) but didn't carry over this fix.

## Fix

One-liner — change the default case to return `None` instead of an error:

```python
case _:
    # Gracefully ignore unknown message types (e.g., rate_limit_event)
    return None
```

This is the same approach used by the SDK. Returning `None` here is safe because the caller in `astream()` already checks `if msg :=` before yielding, so `None` values are naturally filtered out.

Would be great to get this patched — happy to open a PR if that helps!
mlflow/mlflow#21902.title: [BUG] MLflow Assistant: Unknown message type: rate_limit_event

### Willingness to contribute

No. I cannot contribute this feature at this time.

### Proposal Summary

                                                                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  Currently, when logging a PEFT model via `mlflow.transformers.log_model()`, MLflow saves                                                                                                        
  only the adapter weights if the base model originates from HuggingFace Hub                                                                                                                      
  (`_name_or_path` is a Hub ID). In that case, the Hub reference + commit hash is stored                                                                                                          
  and the base model is re-downloaded at load time.                                                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  **The gap:** When the base model is loaded from a local path (e.g., `./models/llama`),                                                                                                          
  MLflow falls back to bundling the full model weights — losing the storage efficiency                                                                                                            
  benefit of adapter-only saving entirely.                                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  **Requested behavior:** Allow users to provide a local base model path reference                                                                                                                
  (similar to how Hub ID + commit hash works), so MLflow saves only the adapter and                                                                                                               
  stores the local path as a reference. At load time, it would resolve the base model                                                                                                             
  from that path.                                                                                                                                                                                 
                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  **Why this matters:**                                                                                                                                                                           
  - Air-gapped / offline environments cannot rely on HF Hub at serve time                                                                                                                         
  - Large base models (7B–70B) make artifact storage impractical when bundled                                                                                                                   
  - Enterprises with internal model stores or data residency requirements need this                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  **Workaround today:** Manually override `model.config._name_or_path` to the Hub ID                                                                                                              
  before logging — which breaks loading in offline environments.

mlflow/mlflow#22066.body:
<!-- issue-warning -->
> [!WARNING]
> Before submitting a PR, please make sure that:
> - A maintainer has triaged this issue and applied the `ready` label
> - This issue has no assignee
> - No duplicate PR exists
>
> PRs not meeting these requirements may be automatically closed.

## Describe the problem

When using the MLflow Assistant with the Claude Code provider, the Claude Code CLI emits `rate_limit_event` messages in its `stream-json` output. The message parser in `ClaudeCodeProvider._parse_message_to_event()` does not handle this message type, causing it to fall through to the catch-all case which converts it into an `Event.from_error()`.

This error event is sent to the frontend via SSE, where the error handler in `AssistantService.ts` closes the `EventSource` connection. This terminates the entire stream before the actual assistant response arrives, making the assistant completely non-functional when rate limit events are present.

### Expected behavior

`rate_limit_event` messages should be silently skipped (like `system` messages), allowing the stream to continue and deliver the actual assistant response.

### Actual behavior

The assistant displays `Error: Unknown message type: rate_limit_event` and never returns a response. The SSE connection is closed on the first unhandled event.

## Root cause

Two contributing factors:

1. **Backend** (`mlflow/assistant/providers/claude_code.py:580-581`): The catch-all case in `_parse_message_to_event()` converts unknown message types into error events:
   ```python
   case _:
       return Event.from_error(f"Unknown message type: {message_type}")
   ```

2. **Frontend** (`mlflow/server/js/src/assistant/AssistantService.ts:250-266`): The SSE error listener closes the EventSource on any error event, terminating the stream:
   ```typescript
   eventSource.addEventListener('error', (event) => {
       // ... parses error and calls onError() ...
       eventSource.close();  // Kills the stream
   });
   ```

## Proposed fix

Add a case for `rate_limit_event` in the message parser to return `None` (skip it), same as `system` messages:

```python
case "rate_limit_event":
    return None
```

## Code to reproduce issue

1. Configure MLflow Assistant with Claude Code provider
2. Start MLflow server: `mlflow server`
3. Open the MLflow UI and send a message to the assistant
4. If the Claude API returns a rate limit event during streaming, the assistant will fail with `Error: Unknown message type: rate_limit_event`

## Affected files

- `mlflow/assistant/providers/claude_code.py` — message parser
- `mlflow/server/js/src/assistant/AssistantService.ts` — SSE error handling
mlflow/mlflow#22066.title: [BUG] Assistant: unhandled rate_limit_event from Claude Code CLI kills SSE stream

### Describe the problem

In the current repo setup, `gateway:/<endpoint>` judge models do not work cleanly in the default GenAI environment. With a natural setup such as `uv sync --extra genai`, users can create and successfully invoke an AI Gateway endpoint, but built-in and custom judges such as `Correctness(model="gateway:/my-endpoint")` or `make_judge(..., model="gateway:/my-endpoint")` fail unless `litellm` is installed. This became easier to hit after `Remove litellm and gepa from genai extras` (#22059), which removed `litellm` from the default GenAI install path and exposed that `gateway:/...` judge execution still relies on `litellm` availability. The expected behavior is that standard offline built-in/custom judge execution through `gateway:/...` works in the default GenAI environment without an extra `litellm` dependency.

## Proposed Fix

Treat `gateway:/<endpoint>` as a native judge backend for standard offline judge execution. Concretely, judge model validation and adapter selection should recognize `gateway:/...` without requiring `litellm`, and built-in/custom judges should route those requests through the existing AI Gateway path directly instead of relying on `litellm` availability. The initial fix can focus on offline built-in and custom judge execution in the default GenAI setup, with regression coverage showing that a working Gateway endpoint is sufficient and that `litellm` is no longer required for this path.

<!-- issue-warning -->

## Summary

When ingesting traces via the OTLP endpoint (`/v1/traces`), the `session.id` span attribute is correctly mapped to `mlflow.trace.session` trace metadata, but `user.id` is **not** mapped to `mlflow.trace.user`. The User column in the MLflow UI remains empty.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Send an OTel trace with `user.id` and `session.id` span attributes to MLflow's OTLP endpoint
2. Check the Traces UI → Session column is populated, User column is empty

Verified with the Python SDK: setting `user.id` as a span attribute does not populate the User column, but using `mlflow.update_current_trace(metadata={"mlflow.trace.user": "..."})` works correctly.

## Root Cause

In `mlflow/store/tracking/sqlalchemy_store.py`, the `log_spans()` method (around line 4594) maps `session.id`:

```python
if session_id is None and (
    span_session_id := span_attributes.get("session.id")
):
    session_id = span_session_id
```

And later persists it as `TraceMetadataKey.TRACE_SESSION` (around line 4697).

However, there is **no equivalent code block** for `user.id` → `TraceMetadataKey.TRACE_USER`.

## Proposed Fix

Add the same pattern for `user.id`, analogous to the `session.id` handling:

```python
# Add alongside session_id initialization (~line 4581):
user_id = None

# Add alongside session.id extraction (~line 4595):
if user_id is None and (
    span_user_id := span_attributes.get("user.id")
):
    user_id = span_user_id

# Add alongside session_id persistence (~line 4714):
if user_id:
    existing_user_id = (
        session.query(SqlTraceMetadata)
        .filter(
            SqlTraceMetadata.request_id == trace_id,
            SqlTraceMetadata.key == TraceMetadataKey.TRACE_USER,
        )
        .one_or_none()
    )
    if not existing_user_id:
        session.merge(
            SqlTraceMetadata(
                request_id=trace_id,
                key=TraceMetadataKey.TRACE_USER,
                value=user_id,
            )
        )
```

## References

- The attribute mapping docs state `user.id` → `mlflow.trace.user`: https://mlflow.org/docs/latest/genai/tracing/opentelemetry/attribute-mapping
- OTel semantic convention: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/registry/attributes/user/#user-id
- `TraceMetadataKey.TRACE_USER` and `SpanRootAttribute.USER_ID` are already defined in `mlflow/tracing/constant.py`

## Environment

- MLflow 3.10.1 (self-hosted via Docker)
- OTel traces sent from Spring AI 2.0.0-M4 / Spring Boot 4.0.2

## Summary

The Mistral AI Gateway provider sets `NAME = "Mistral"` (capitalized), which is passed to LiteLLM as `custom_llm_provider`. LiteLLM's `cost_per_token()` is case-sensitive and only recognizes `"mistral"` (lowercase), causing cost calculation to fail silently.

This affects both:
- AI Gateway cost tracking (Usage tab shows no costs)
- Budget tracker cost recording

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure an AI Gateway endpoint with Mistral provider
2. Send requests through the gateway
3. Check the Usage tab → no costs displayed

## Root Cause

In `mlflow/gateway/providers/mistral.py` line 201:

```python
NAME = "Mistral"  # Capitalized
```

This is used in `base.py` line 247 as `SpanAttributeKey.MODEL_PROVIDER`, then passed to `calculate_cost_by_model_and_token_usage()` in `tracing/utils/__init__.py` line 345 as `custom_llm_provider`.

LiteLLM is case-sensitive:

```python
>>> cost_per_token(model="mistral-large-latest", custom_llm_provider="Mistral")
# FAILS: "This model isn't mapped yet"

>>> cost_per_token(model="mistral-large-latest", custom_llm_provider="mistral")
# WORKS: (4.99e-05, 7.5e-05)
```

## Proposed Fix

```python
# mlflow/gateway/providers/mistral.py
NAME = "mistral"  # lowercase to match LiteLLM's provider registry
```

Or alternatively, lowercase the provider name before passing to LiteLLM in `calculate_cost_by_model_and_token_usage()`.

## Environment

- MLflow 3.10.1 (self-hosted via Docker)
- LiteLLM 1.82.0
mlflow/mlflow#22120.title: AI Gateway: Mistral provider NAME case mismatch breaks LiteLLM cost calculation

mlflow/mlflow#22124.body: > [!WARNING] > Before submitting a PR, please make sure that: > - A maintainer has triaged this issue and applied the `ready` label > - This issue has no assignee > - No duplicate PR exists > > PRs not meeting these requirements may be automatically closed. ### MLflow version 3.11.1.dev0 (built from source, master branch) ### System information - **OS Platform and Distribution**: macOS Darwin 25.3.0 - **Python version**: 3.10 ### Describe the problem The dataset details drawer (opened from the run overview or the Models table Dataset column) has three issues when displaying datasets logged via `mlflow.log_input()`: #### 1. "Open dataset" button navigates to unrelated evaluation datasets page The "Open dataset" button links to `/experiments/{id}/datasets`, which is the **evaluation datasets** tab (GenAI feature, `ajax-api/3.0/mlflow/datasets/search`). This page has no connection to run-logged datasets and shows "No evaluation datasets found." **Root cause:** `ExperimentViewDatasetLink.tsx` unconditionally links to `ExperimentPageTabName.Datasets` when `experimentId` is present, regardless of dataset type. #### 2. Local source type and path not displayed For datasets with `source_type: "local"`, the source type label and source URI are not shown. Both `ExperimentViewDatasetSourceType.tsx` and `ExperimentViewDatasetSourceURL.tsx` only handle HTTP, S3, and Hugging Face — they return `null` for `local`. The path (e.g., `/data/photos/my-concept`) is stored on the server in `dataset.source` but never rendered. #### 3. Profile displayed as raw JSON The profile field is displayed as a truncated raw JSON string instead of being parsed and rendered as structured key-value pairs. For example, an AI training toolkit may send rich dataset metadata in the profile: ```json { "num_images": 50, "resolution": "1024x1024", "has_captions": true, "caption_ext": ".txt", "trigger_word": "ohwx", "format": "image_folder", "total_size_mb": 312.5 } ``` But the drawer renders it as: `{"num_images": 50, "resolution": "1024x1024", "has_captions": true, "caption_ext ...` ### Steps to reproduce the bug **Step 1:** Log a dataset and open the run page. Click the dataset in the "Datasets" section. **Step 2:** The drawer opens. Note: profile is raw JSON, no source type/path shown below the digest. **Step 3:** Click "Open dataset". It navigates to an empty evaluation datasets page. ### Code to generate data required to reproduce the bug ```python import mlflow import pandas as pd mlflow.set_experiment("dataset-drawer-bug-repro") with mlflow.start_run(run_name="lora-training-run"): mlflow.log_param("base_model", "black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-dev") mlflow.log_param("lora_rank", 16) mlflow.log_param("learning_rate", "1e-4") df = pd.DataFrame({ "image_path": [f"img_{i:03d}.jpg" for i in range(50)], "caption": [f"a photo of ohwx person {i}" for i in range(50)], "resolution": ["1024x1024"] * 50, }) dataset = mlflow.data.from_pandas(df, name="my-concept-photos", source="/data/photos/my-concept") # AI toolkit sends rich metadata in the profile field rich_profile = { "num_images": 50, "resolution": "1024x1024", "has_captions": True, "caption_ext": ".txt", "trigger_word": "ohwx", "format": "image_folder", "total_size_mb": 312.5, } dataset.__class__.profile = property(lambda self: rich_profile) mlflow.log_input(dataset, context="training") for step in range(10): mlflow.log_metric("loss", 1.0 - step * 0.09, step=step) ``` ### Is the console panel in DevTools showing errors relevant to the bug? Type 'N/A' if not applicable. N/A — no console errors related to these issues. ### Does the network panel in DevTools contain failed requests relevant to the bug? Type 'N/A' if not applicable. N/A — clicking "Open dataset" triggers a successful request to the evaluation datasets API which returns empty results: ``` # Request URL http://localhost:5000/ajax-api/3.0/mlflow/datasets/search # Status Code 200 # Payload {"experiment_ids": ["14"], "order_by": ["created_time DESC"], "max_results": 50} # Response {} ```
mlflow/mlflow#22124.title: [BUG] Dataset details drawer: broken "Open dataset" link, missing local source info, raw JSON profile

mlflow/mlflow#22142.body:
<!-- issue-warning -->
> [!WARNING]
> Before submitting a PR, please make sure that:
> - A maintainer has triaged this issue and applied the `ready` label
> - This issue has no assignee
> - No duplicate PR exists
>
> PRs not meeting these requirements may be automatically closed.

### Issues Policy acknowledgement

- [x] I have read and agree to submit bug reports in accordance with the [issues policy](https://www.github.com/mlflow/mlflow/blob/master/ISSUE_POLICY.md)

### Where did you encounter this bug?

Other

### MLflow version

- Client: 3.8.1 (also verified in 3.10.1 via source inspection)
- Tracking server: 3.8.1

### System information

- OS Platform and Distribution: Linux (Kubernetes, Docker container)
- Python version: 3.13

### Describe the problem

When using `mlflow server` with `--static-prefix=/<prefix>`, the `/api/2.0/mlflow/` REST API routes
are not prefixed, while the `/ajax-api/2.0/mlflow/` routes are correctly prefixed.

**Root cause:** In `mlflow/server/handlers.py`, `_get_rest_path()` does NOT call `_add_static_prefix()`,
while `_get_ajax_path()` does:

    def _get_rest_path(base_path, version=2):
        return f"/api/{version}.0{base_path}"              # ← missing _add_static_prefix()

    def _get_ajax_path(base_path, version=2):
        return _add_static_prefix(f"/ajax-api/{version}.0{base_path}")  # ← has _add_static_prefix()

**Expected behavior:**
When starting the server with `mlflow server --static-prefix=/myprefix`, ALL of the following should work:
- `/myprefix/api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/search` → 200
- `/myprefix/ajax-api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/search` → 200

**Actual behavior:**
- `/myprefix/api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/search` → **404 Not Found**
- `/myprefix/ajax-api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/search` → 200 ✅
- `/api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/search` (without prefix) → 200 ✅

This means `--static-prefix` only applies to `ajax-api` routes but not `api` routes.

**Suggested fix** (one-line change in `mlflow/server/handlers.py`):

    def _get_rest_path(base_path, version=2):
        return _add_static_prefix(f"/api/{version}.0{base_path}")

### Tracking information

N/A - this is a server-side routing issue, not a client tracking issue.

### Code to reproduce issue

```bash
# Start MLflow with --static-prefix
mlflow server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5000 --static-prefix=/myapp

# These work:
curl http://localhost:5000/myapp/health                                          # 200 OK
curl http://localhost:5000/myapp/ajax-api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/search?max_results=1  # 200 OK
curl http://localhost:5000/api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/search?max_results=1       # 200 OK (no prefix)

# This does NOT work (but should):
curl http://localhost:5000/myapp/api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/search?max_results=1  # 404 Not Found
```

### Stack trace

<!-- PLEASE KEEP BACKTICKS AND CHECK PREVIEW -->
```
REPLACE_ME
```


### Other info / logs

This bug affects anyone deploying MLflow tracking server behind a reverse proxy
with path-based routing (e.g., Kubernetes ingress with multiple MLflow instances
under different path prefixes).

The issue has been present since at least MLflow 3.7.0 and is still present
in 3.10.1 (verified by inspecting the source code of the PyPI wheel).

Workaround: Use nginx configuration-snippet to rewrite the old API path:
    rewrite ^/(<prefix>)/api/(.*)$ /$1/ajax-api/$2 break;

### What component(s) does this bug affect?

- [x] `area/tracking`: Tracking Service, tracking client APIs, autologging
- [ ] `area/model-registry`: Model Registry service, APIs, and the fluent client calls for Model Registry
- [ ] `area/scoring`: MLflow model serving, deployment tools, Spark UDFs
- [ ] `area/evaluation`: MLflow model evaluation features, evaluation metrics, and evaluation workflows
- [ ] `area/prompt`: MLflow prompt engineering features, prompt templates, and prompt management
- [ ] `area/tracing`: MLflow Tracing features, tracing APIs, and LLM tracing functionality
- [ ] `area/gateway`: MLflow AI Gateway client APIs, server, and third-party integrations
- [ ] `area/projects`: MLproject format, project running backends
- [ ] `area/uiux`: Front-end, user experience, plotting
- [ ] `area/docs`: MLflow documentation pages
mlflow/mlflow#22142.title: [BUG] --static-prefix not applied to /api/2.0/mlflow/ routes (_get_rest_path missing _add_static_prefix)

modal-labs/modal-client#3893.body:
Hi Modal team,

I've built an MLflow deployment plugin that enables one-command deployment of MLflow models to Modal:

**Repository**: https://github.com/debu-sinha/mlflow-modal-deploy
**PyPI**: https://pypi.org/project/mlflow-modal-deploy/

**Features**:
- Full MLflow `BaseDeploymentClient` implementation (create, update, delete, list, get, predict)
- GPU support (T4, L4, A10G, A100, A100-80GB, H100)
- Dynamic batching and auto-scaling configuration
- Automatic dependency detection from model artifacts
- Wheel file support for private dependencies

**Usage**:
```bash
pip install mlflow-modal-deploy
mlflow deployments create -t modal -m runs:/abc123/model --name my-model -C gpu=T4
```

```python
from mlflow.deployments import get_deploy_client

client = get_deploy_client("modal")
client.create_deployment(
    name="my-classifier",
    model_uri="runs:/abc123/model",
    config={"gpu": "T4", "enable_batching": True}
)
```

The MLflow maintainers suggested building this as a standalone plugin (similar to `mlflow-txtai`).

**Questions**:
1. Would you be interested in co-maintaining or officially endorsing the plugin?
2. Would you consider listing it in Modal's documentation or integrations page?

Happy to collaborate on any improvements or align with Modal's roadmap.
modal-labs/modal-client#3893.title: MLflow Deployment Plugin for Modal
